title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Easy Butter Chicken Recipe: Authentic Murgh Makhani
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a delicious homemade meal. Get cooking!
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani step by step
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to be served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian dish perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian classic known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Tender pieces of marinated chicken are simmered in a luscious gravy, subtly spiced and finished with a touch of butter and cream. This iconic dish offers a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors, making it a truly satisfying culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 mins, then pureed with a little water

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover the bowl and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to tenderize the chicken and infuse flavors.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter. Once the butter is melted and shimmering, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Sear the chicken until lightly browned on all sides. The chicken does not need to be cooked through at this stage. Remove the seared chicken from the pan and set it aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter. Reduce the heat to medium. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté, stirring occasionally, until it becomes translucent and lightly golden brown, which should take about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1/2 tablespoon ginger garlic paste and the slit green chilies (if using) and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes to the pan, along with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Stir well to combine all ingredients. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then cover the pan and let it cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally. This allows the tomatoes to cook down, thicken the sauce, and deepen the flavors, removing any raw tomato taste.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Cashew Paste
instructions.text: Stir the pureed cashews and granulated sugar into the simmering tomato gravy. Return the seared chicken pieces to the pan. Pour in about 1/2 cup of water or chicken broth; add more if needed to achieve your desired sauce consistency. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer again, cover, and cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is completely tender and cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish with Butter and Cream
instructions.text: Remove the pan from the heat. Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter, the crushed dried fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi), and the heavy cream. Gently mix until the butter is melted and the cream is fully incorporated, making the sauce rich and smooth. Do not boil the sauce after adding the cream. Taste and adjust the seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish the Butter Chicken generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with your favorite Indian breads like naan or roti, or alongside fluffy basmati rice for a complete and satisfying meal.

tips: For truly tender chicken, marinate it for at least 2-4 hours, or even overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat and infuse deep flavors.
Do not skip the Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves); it provides an essential, authentic aroma and flavor characteristic of traditional Butter Chicken.
To achieve an extra smooth sauce, you can blend the tomato-onion gravy with an immersion blender or cool and blend in a regular blender before adding the chicken and cream.
article:
Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is more than just a dish; it's a culinary hug, a vibrant symphony of flavors that has captivated palates worldwide. This iconic Indian lunch recipe, known for its velvety smooth texture, rich tomato-based sauce, and succulent pieces of marinated chicken, is the epitome of comfort food. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore the wonders of Indian cuisine, mastering Murgh Makhani is a truly rewarding experience. Get ready to embark on a flavorful journey as we delve into the secrets of creating the perfect Butter Chicken, a dish that promises to transform your midday meal into an extraordinary feast.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

At its heart, Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, this dish was serendipitously created by Kundan Lal Jaggi at Moti Mahal restaurant. It was born out of a desire to use leftover tandoori chicken by simmering it in a rich, creamy tomato gravy. The result was a revelation: tender, smoky chicken bathed in a luxurious sauce that balances sweetness, tang, and a subtle warmth from carefully selected spices.

What makes Murgh Makhani so universally beloved is its harmonious blend of flavors. Unlike many Indian curries that boast fiery heat, Butter Chicken is notably mild, making it approachable for all spice tolerances. The sauce is typically made from pureed tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a host of aromatic spices like garam masala, cumin, and coriander. Crucially, copious amounts of butter and cream are folded in at the end, lending it that signature silkiness and richness. It’s a dish that embodies indulgence and comfort, making it an ideal choice for a satisfying Indian lunch that leaves you feeling nourished and content.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken requires a few key components, each playing a vital role in building its complex flavor profile. Don't be intimidated by the list; most ingredients are readily available.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28 ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (optional, for extra creaminess and thickness)
*   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1/2 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon sugar (to balance acidity)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt, or to taste
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (or half-and-half)
*   1 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Instructions

Creating the perfect Butter Chicken at home is an achievable feat with these clear, concise steps.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably for 2-4 hours, or even overnight, for maximum flavor and tenderness.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a little oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook until the chicken is lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set the cooked chicken aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan (or a clean pot), melt the butter with the oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Simmer the Tomatoes and Spices:** Add the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree), cashews (if using), red chili powder, garam masala, cumin, and coriander to the pan. Mix well. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the raw tomato flavor mellows.

5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the cooked sauce mixture to a blender. If you added cashews, ensure they are fully softened. Blend until the sauce is completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot.

6.  **Finish the Murgh Makhani Sauce:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot over low heat. Stir in the sugar and salt. Add the heavy cream and the crushed kasuri methi. Simmer gently for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld beautifully. Do not boil the sauce once the cream is added.

7.  **Combine and Serve:** Add the pre-cooked chicken pieces to the creamy sauce. Stir gently to coat the chicken and heat through for 2-3 minutes. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready to be devoured!

## Pro Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is within reach with these expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will become, absorbing all those wonderful spices and yogurt.
*   **Quality Tomatoes Matter:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes for consistent results, or ensure your fresh tomatoes are very ripe and sweet. This forms the backbone of your sauce.
*   **The Power of Blending:** For that signature silky smooth sauce, blending is non-negotiable. Don't skip this step, and blend until there are absolutely no lumps. Straining optional for extra smoothness.
*   **Kasuri Methi is Essential:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) impart a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor that is crucial to authentic Murgh Makhani. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their oils and fragrance.
*   **Don't Skimp on Butter and Cream:** These ingredients are not just for richness; they contribute significantly to the dish's characteristic texture and mildness. They balance the acidity of the tomatoes and the warmth of the spices.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** Taste your sauce before adding the chicken. Adjust salt, sugar, and chili powder as needed. A little sugar helps cut through the tomato's acidity, while a pinch more chili powder can add a gentle kick.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch.

*   **Bread:** The classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. Tandoori roti, chapati, or paratha are also excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a comforting base that soaks up the creamy gravy beautifully.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad, a dollop of cooling raita (yogurt dip), or a spicy pickle can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Garnish:** A final swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro not only adds visual appeal but also enhances the overall flavor and aroma.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences.

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this dish into a vegetarian delight by substituting chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or a medley of vegetables like cauliflower, peas, and bell peppers. Simply sauté them before adding to the sauce.
*   **Healthier Options:** For a lighter version, you can reduce the amount of butter and cream. Use low-fat yogurt in the marinade and opt for light coconut milk or a cashew-almond paste (soaked and blended nuts) instead of heavy cream for a dairy-free alternative, though this will slightly alter the traditional flavor profile. Lean chicken breast can also replace thighs.
*   **Spice Level Adjustment:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder in the sauce. For a milder take, use less or stick to Kashmiri chili powder, which offers color without much heat.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** To mimic the slight smokiness of tandoor-cooked chicken, you can grill or broil your marinated chicken until it gets some charred bits before adding it to the sauce. Alternatively, a pinch of smoked paprika can be added to the sauce.
*   **Nut-Free Version:** If you have a nut allergy, omit the cashews. The sauce will still be delicious, though perhaps slightly less thick. You can compensate with a little extra cream or by simmering the sauce a bit longer.

Butter Chicken is a testament to the magic of Indian cuisine – a dish that’s both simple and sophisticated, comforting and celebratory. Its enduring popularity as a beloved Indian lunch recipe is no surprise, offering a harmonious balance of flavors that satisfies and delights. Don't hesitate to dive in and create this magnificent Murgh Makhani in your own kitchen. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and most importantly, relish every creamy, flavorful bite.
